_comment: Copy this to runtime_config.json and set model_filename. Run agent/first_run.py to auto-generate this for your hardware.
_section_minimum_operator: Minimum keys for a usable daily driver: (1) model_filename OR remote inference via ollama_base_url / llama_server_url + inference_backend; (2) sandbox_root — operator home or dedicated folder; (3) n_ctx sized to your GPU/RAM; (4) max_tool_calls + max_runtime_seconds aligned with UI timeouts. Optional: workspace_awareness_auto_enabled + workspace_awareness_debounce_seconds; autonomous_mode for POST /autonomous/run; use_chroma false on weak hardware. Full acceptance: docs/GOLDEN_FLOW.md section 10.
_comment_models_dir: Optional key: models_dir (string path to folder of .gguf files). Omit or remove from a copied config to use the default. With LAYLA_DATA_DIR, defaults are under that data directory; first-run GET /setup_status also lists .gguf files in the repo-root models/ folder (beside agent/) so source installs can keep models in-repo without editing config.
_comment_speculative_decoding: Enable llama.cpp prompt-lookup speculative decoding when supported by llama-cpp-python. Can improve throughput; safe to leave enabled because unsupported versions ignore it via llama-cpp-python TypeError fallback.
_comment_speculative_num_pred_tokens: Speculative decoding: number of tokens to draft per iteration. Recommended: 10 on GPU, 2 on CPU-only.
_comment_max_runtime_seconds: Wall-clock cap per agent turn (seconds). Default matches ui_agent_stream_timeout_seconds so the server rarely stops before the browser.
_comment_chat_light_max_runtime_seconds: Cap for short non-tool chat (see agent_loop _is_lightweight_chat_turn). Keep ≥60 for slow CPU/GGUF.
_comment_ui_agent_stream_timeout_seconds: Browser wait cap for streaming POST /agent (seconds). Increase on slow CPU/GGUF or when performance_mode is low.
_comment_ui_agent_json_timeout_seconds: Browser wait cap for non-stream /agent and /execute_plan JSON responses.
_comment_ui_stream_keepalive_seconds: Streaming /agent: SSE pulse interval during silence (resets stall warning). 0 disables.
_comment_ui_stalled_silence_ms: 0 = derive stall warning from max_runtime_seconds × performance_mode (streaming UI only).
_comment_honesty_and_boundaries_enabled: Adds an integrity block to the system head: may disagree kindly, no flattery/manipulation/human pretense/hidden actions.
_comment_context_protection_threshold: Proactive conversation compression threshold as a fraction of n_ctx (applied before each decision). Lower = compress earlier to protect weak models.
_comment_step_summarization_threshold: When steps exceed this count, the agent injects a deterministic compressed summary into the decision prompt to reduce context overload.
_comment_system_head_budget_ratio: Fraction of n_ctx reserved for system head assembly when prompt_budget_enabled (cap 0.15–0.55).
_section_context_budgets: Tiered caps for memory/knowledge/workspace in system head (see services/prompt_tier_budget.py).
_comment_tool_visibility_cap: Max tool names shown to the model per decision (confusion reduction). Capped to 8–30 in code.
_comment_deterministic_tool_routes_enabl: When true, deterministically narrow tools by task type (coding/research/planning/file_ops) before intent filtering.
_comment_deterministic_tool_routes: Optional override for deterministic tool routes. Keys: coding|research|planning|file_ops -> [tool names].
_comment_decision_few_shot_enabled: When true, the decision prompt appends a few-shot block with valid one-line JSON examples (improves small-model reliability).
_comment_deterministic_tool_verification: When true, deterministic semantic verification runs after tools (file exists, returncode==0, non-empty output). May downgrade ok->false if verification fails.
When deterministic verification fails on selected tools, automatically retry the tool once with the same args.
_comment_llm_tool_verification_enabled: When true, an LLM-based progress check runs after certain tools (optional; deterministic checks always run first).
_comment_structured_retry_enabled: When true, in-loop planning retries follow a 3-level ladder (debug context -> simplified plan -> model switch).
_comment_structured_retry_max_levels: Max retry levels for structured retry ladder (1-3).
_comment_max_plan_steps: Plan pre-validation caps plan length to this many steps (clamped 1-12). Pre-validation currently always runs when planning is used.
_comment_validation_matrix_enabled: When true, outcome evaluation includes a deterministic multi-dimensional validation matrix.
_comment_completion_gate_enabled: When true, the agent retries instead of returning low-quality final text; may return structured failure when exhausted.
_comment_remote_cors_origins: When non-empty, FastAPI adds CORSMiddleware for these origins (tunnel / phone browser).
_comment_cloudflared_path: Optional full path to cloudflared.exe / cloudflared binary for POST /remote/tunnel/start.
_comment_autonomous_research_v2: Autonomous Research v2: Tier-0-only multi-step read-only investigations. Local-only by default. POST /autonomous/run requires confirm_autonomous=true. Remote access requires remote_enabled=true and explicit remote_allow_endpoints allowlisting for /autonomous/run.
_comment_autonomous_wiki_enabled: Wiki module toggle for markdown writes (sandboxed under workspace .layla/wiki).
_comment_autonomous_wiki_export_enabled: When true WITH autonomous_wiki_enabled, POST /autonomous/run may append wiki markdown after high-confidence fresh runs (router sets allow_write).
_comment_autonomous_prefetch_enabled: When true, match goal against investigation_reuse.jsonl, then wiki *.md, then Chroma learnings, before running the planner.
_comment_autonomous_reuse_match_threshol: Jaccard-style overlap threshold (0–1) for JSONL prefetch.
_comment_autonomous_wiki_match_threshold: Overlap threshold for wiki markdown prefetch.
_comment_autonomous_chroma: Read-only prefetch from Chroma learnings collection (requires use_chroma). Cosine similarity threshold on best hit.
_comment_autonomous_read_cache: Tier-0 read_file LRU shared across POST /autonomous/run calls (invalidates on mtime/size change).
_comment_investigation_reuse_store_enabl: When true, append high-confidence investigation summary + findings to workspace .layla/investigation_reuse.jsonl.
_comment_fabrication_assist: Fabrication Assist deterministic kernel. Default runner is StubRunner (no subprocess). To allow SubprocessJsonRunner (explicit plan-step request only), set fabrication_assist.enable_subprocess=true.
_comment_response_cache_enabled: Caches repeated short non-tool chat requests in memory for instant replies.
_comment_telemetry_log_trivial: When false, skip DB telemetry rows for reasoning_mode=none (reduces simple-turn ratio pollution).
_comment_model_outcome_tracking_enabled: When true, store model outcome rows in SQLite (model_outcomes) for adaptive routing bias. Local-only; no network.
_comment_sandbox_python_memory_limit_mb: 0 = unlimited. On Linux/macOS sets RLIMIT_AS for the child process; ignored on Windows.
_comment_max_patch_lines: 0 = unlimited. When set, apply_patch rejects patches with more newline-separated lines.
_comment_file_checkpoint: When true, write_file / apply_patch / search_replace / write_files_batch snapshot prior file contents under workspace .layla/file_checkpoints/ (list_file_checkpoints, restore_file_checkpoint).
_comment_elasticsearch: Optional mirror of learnings to Elasticsearch 8.x for keyword search (pip install elasticsearch). SQLite + Chroma remain source of truth.
_comment_learning_min_score: Minimum persisted learning score to inject into prompts.
_comment_auto_lint_test_fix_ruff_fix: When omitted from runtime_config.json, code uses explicit bool if set; else ruff --fix runs only when workspace_root is inside the Layla source tree (see runtime_safety.effective_auto_lint_test_fix_ruff_fix). Example keeps true for permissive templates.
_comment_research_max_runtime_seconds: Research-mode agent turns; default in code is 1800s if unset. Example uses 900s for moderate sessions.
_comment_mcp_client: MCP stdio client: services/mcp_client.py + agent tool mcp_tools_call (short session per call). Enable only with trusted servers; requires allow_run and normal dangerous-tool approvals.
_comment_mcp_prompt: When mcp_client_enabled, optionally inject a short TTL-cached MCP tools/list summary into the decision prompt (saves tokens vs listing every tool in system head).
_comment_agent_hooks: Claude-Code-style lifecycle hooks: subprocess per entry. pre_tool/post_tool respect hooks_require_allow_run unless false. session_start runs whenever agent_hooks_enabled.
_comment_background_use_subprocess_worke: When true, /agent/background and /agents/spawn run autonomous_run in a child process (hard cancel via terminate/kill). Default false: in-process thread + cooperative client_abort_event. Each worker uses the same runtime_config.json: with local llama_cpp it loads its own GGUF (N× RAM). Point llama_server_url or ollama_base_url at a shared inference server so workers stay thin HTTP clients.
_comment_background_subprocess_local_ggu: When background_use_subprocess_workers and inference resolve to in-process llama_cpp: warn (log), reject (block enqueue), or allow (silent). Use reject in production if you require shared inference only.
_comment_background_worker_force_sandbox: If true, reject spawn when workspace_root resolves outside sandbox_root.
_comment_background_worker_rlimits_enabl: Linux/macOS worker only: set RLIMIT_AS before loading llama_cpp. Best-effort — mmap-heavy GGUF may not respect AS; prefer systemd/cgroups/Docker memory limits (see docs/RUNBOOKS.md).
_comment_background_worker_rlimit_as_byt: Virtual address space cap for worker process when background_worker_rlimits_enabled. Example 12GiB: 12884901888
_comment_background_worker_windows_job_l: Attach worker to a Windows Job Object with a memory cap (ctypes). Best-effort; test on your build.
_comment_background_worker_windows_job_c: When background_worker_windows_job_limits_enabled: optional Job Object CPU hard cap (1–100). No-op on older Windows if unsupported.
_comment_background_worker_rlimit_cpu_se: POSIX worker: optional RLIMIT_CPU (wall-clock style, coarse). Prefer Linux cgroup cpu.max when using background_worker_cgroup_auto_enabled.
_comment_background_progress_stream_enab: When true, background thread/subprocess tasks emit throttled progress events (tool steps) to progress_json / GET /agent/tasks/{id} as progress_events.
_comment_background_progress_tail_max: Max events included in task API field progress_tail (full list also in progress / progress_events).
_comment_background_job_max_stderr_bytes: Cap stderr read from background_job_worker (NDJSON progress lines + logs).
_comment_background_worker_cgroup_auto_e: Linux only: create cgroup v2 leaf under parent and set memory.max / cpu.max for subprocess worker. Requires delegation (systemd/container).
_comment_background_worker_cgroup_cpu_ma: cgroup v2 cpu.max string, e.g. "50000 100000" for 50% of one CPU.
_comment_background_worker_wrapper_comma: Optional argv prefix for the worker, e.g. bubblewrap or firejail — operator-supplied; Layla does not ship a profile. Example: ["bwrap", "--ro-bind", "/usr", "/usr", ...]
_comment_geometry_frameworks_enabled: Toggle optional kernels used by layla.geometry and tools geometry_*.
_comment_geometry_external_bridge_url: Optional base URL for operator-hosted CAD bridge (POST JSON). Used by cad_bridge_fetch and gencad_generate_toolpath (body: op, file, strategy, workspace_root).
_comment_memory_retrieval_min_adjusted_c: Filter semantic/FTS memory recall rows below this adjusted_confidence (0 = off). See North Star learning judgment.
_comment_inline_initiative_enabled: Append one heuristic suggestion line after multi-tool replies (default off).
_comment_initiative_engine_enabled: When true, merge rule-based initiative hints from services/initiative_engine.py into inline suggestions and optional wakeup lines (suggestions only).
_comment_initiative_project_proposals_en: When true, initiative_engine may generate 1-3 text-only project proposals from `.layla/project_memory.json` (gated by autonomy trust tier >= 2).
_comment_autonomy_optimizer_enabled: When true, governed plan step retries add text-only recovery hints from services/autonomy_optimizer.py (never widens allow_write/allow_run).
_comment_autonomy_trust_tiers_enabled: When true, maturity rank derives a conservative autonomy trust tier (0-3). Tier 3 is operator override only; used to gate risky autonomy like multi-attempt coordinator retries.
_comment_trust_tier_override: Optional operator override for trust tier (0-3). Leave null for automatic (rank-based) tiers. Can also be stored in user_identity as trust_tier_override.
_comment_codex_semantic_enabled: When true, services/codex_semantic.py can rank codex proposals by token overlap (optional Chroma path later).
_comment_voice_adjustment_inject_enabled: When true, inject user_identity key voice_adjustment into system personality block (capped).
_comment_output_quality_gate_enabled: When true, apply deterministic output cleanup (reduce hedges, dedupe paragraphs) in services/output_polish.py.
_comment_observation_mode_enabled: When true, nascent maturity phase biases the agent to prefer reasoning over tools unless explicitly requested (agent_loop decision prompt).
_comment_capability_level_inject_enabled: When true, inject capability training snapshot into the system head for better tool choice and self-awareness.
_comment_maturity_enabled: Enable Layla v3 maturity/XP system hooks (services/maturity_engine.py).
_comment_project_discovery_auto_inject: When project memory is sparse, inject deterministic workspace scan brief into system head (discover_project, not LLM run_project_discovery).
_comment_planning_outcome_bias_enabled: When true (default), create_plan prompt includes last outcome score, recent tool failures, persona planner nudges, and toolchain cost/risk hints.
_comment_relationship_codex_inject: When true, a short digest from workspace `.layla/relationship_codex.json` is appended to system context (local operator data). Cap with relationship_codex_inject_max_chars.
_comment_engineering_pipeline: When engineering_pipeline_enabled=true: use POST /agent engineering_pipeline_mode chat|plan|execute; plan uses blocking clarifier + planner; execute runs full pipeline (see docs/STRUCTURED_ENGINEERING_PARTNER.md). clarification_reply continues after needs_input.
_comment_self_reflection_min_length: Minimum response length to trigger the critic pass when enable_self_reflection=true (saves latency on short replies).
_comment_golden_examples_enabled: When true, store and inject small high-score golden examples (few-shot patterns) from prior successful runs.
_comment_benchmark_on_load: When true: model bench on first LLM load; also runs lightweight embedding + vector_search capability benchmarks in a background thread at server startup.
_comment_embedder_prewarm_enabled: Optional startup warmup for embedding model. Keep false for max startup stability on Python 3.14 + torchao.
_comment_performance_mode: low | mid | high | auto — auto maps from RAM/GPU tiers via system_optimizer.get_effective_config() (runtime only).
_comment_potato_preset: Web UI Settings → Apply potato preset, or POST /settings/preset {"preset":"potato"} — merges low caps + use_chroma false + smaller n_ctx; see docs/POTATO_MODE.md
_comment_models: Optional aliases: code=magicoder maps to a known GGUF basename under models_dir. coding_model / reasoning_model / chat_model still override when set.
_comment_model_fallback_chain: Optional: ordered list of GGUF basenames to try when the routed model file is missing. The system always tries model_filename last.
_comment_architecture_overhaul: Coordinator, pipeline stages, task persistence, background intelligence — see ARCHITECTURE.md
_comment_remote: Set remote_enabled=true and bind uvicorn to 0.0.0.0 to allow phone/LAN access. Set remote_api_key to a secret string. Visit /ui from your phone using the URL shown in Help > Phone Access.
_comment_remote_rate_limit_per_minute: When remote_enabled, max HTTP requests per minute per non-localhost IP (0 = unlimited).
_comment_inference_backend: auto: no URL -> llama_cpp (local GGUF); Ollama URL -> ollama; else openai_compatible. See docs/OPENCLAW_ALIGNMENT.md
_comment_decision_model: Optional GGUF basename dedicated to decision JSON generation (tool/think/reason). Use a small structured-output model (Phi/Hermes/Qwen) to reduce JSON parsing failures.
_comment_github_repo: owner/repo used for update checks against GitHub Releases.
_comment_ui_theme_preset: Optional hint for Web UI theming (first_run wizard); UI may read localStorage first.
_comment_response_pacing_ms: Minimum milliseconds between successive streamed chunks in the final reply (stream_reason / SSE). 0 = off; gaps capped at 10s server-side.
_comment_repo_cognition: After POST /workspace/cognition/sync (or tool sync_repo_cognition), inject stored digests into the system head for listed workspace roots + project cognition_extra_roots.
_comment_project_memory: Per-workspace `.layla/project_memory.json`: structural scan + optional plan/todos. Injected into system head when file exists (bounded by project_memory_inject_max_chars). Tools: scan_repo, update_project_memory (gated). understand_mode on POST /agent writes memory + syncs cognition.
_comment_planning_strict_mode: When true, mutating tools (dangerous registry + shell/run/mcp/tests/pip/git commit) require plan_approved: pass plan_id on POST /agent after POST /plans/{id}/approve, use POST /plans/{id}/execute, or POST /execute_plan (implicit approval). Exceptions: scan_repo, update_project_memory for repo mapping.
_comment_decision_policy: Merge outcome/cognitive_workspace/toolchain signals into PolicyCaps; clamps effective tool list each decision tick.
_comment_plan_system_first: Rule-based skeleton plans before LLM; plan_llm_gap_fill_only fills OPEN steps only.
_comment_chat_lite_mode: Shorter path: disables macro-planning and cognitive workspace for this process (in-memory cfg overlay).
_comment_task_budget: When true, profile_task + allocate_budget cap max_tool_calls (after token-pressure), max_plan_depth, retrieval depth, and macro_planning_allowed for this run. See docs/ADAPTIVE_EXECUTION_ENGINE.md.
_comment_force_full_pipeline: If true, skip the trivial-turn quick_reply path so short greetings always run the full loop (debug/CI).
_comment_run_budget_summary_log_enabled: Emit structured log event run_budget_summary per autonomous_run (wall time, token estimates, tool count, pipeline_variant).
_comment_api_confidence_enabled: Attach non-calibrated confidence object to /agent JSON from outcome_evaluation heuristic.
_comment_langfuse: Optional cloud traces: pip install langfuse; set keys. No-op if disabled or import fails.
_comment_ui_appearance: Optional UI / character wizard fields (merged via POST /settings/appearance).
_comment_in_loop_plan_governance: When true (default), should_plan → create_plan → execute_plan uses step_governance=True (same validation/retries as POST /execute_plan). Nested autonomous_run gets plan_approved if outer plan_approved or allow_write or allow_run (avoids planning_strict_mode deadlock). Set false to restore legacy in-loop behavior.
_comment_plan_governance_require_nonempt: When true, approve fails if edit/test/build/refactor/cad steps have empty tools[]; with in_loop_plan_governance_enabled, create_plan steps get default read-only tools (plan_step_default_read_tools) only for analysis-like rows (marked _tools_auto_filled).
_comment_plan_governance_reject_auto_fil: When true, governed steps whose tools[] were auto-filled (in-loop normalize) fail validation immediately — operator must list tools explicitly in the plan.
_comment_plan_governance_strict_tool_evi: When true, edit/test step validation inspects tool result dicts (paths, pytest output, return codes), not only assistant prose. Implied when plan_governance_hard_mode is true.
_comment_plan_governance_hard_mode: When true, enables strict tool evidence + reject auto-filled tools + require nonempty tools on mutating steps (same as turning those three flags on).
_comment_llm_serialize_per_workspace: When true, agent runs hold a per-workspace lock; local llama_cpp uses a separate global generation lock (two workspaces can interleave tool work; one GPU generate at a time). Default false preserves legacy single global agent lock.
_comment_file_plan_step_tools: When a file-backed plan step lists step.tools[], the agent loop rejects any other tool (including batch_tools) for that autonomous_run. Empty step.tools = no per-step tool restriction (other gates still apply).
_comment_file_plan_refinement: When true, file-plan executor runs one optional LLM pass to tighten outputs for steps with type planning (extra cost).
_comment_dual_models: Boss/minion: set chat_model or models.fast (fast) + model_filename or coding_model (heavy). When free RAM >= dual_model_threshold_gb (or force_dual_models=true), chat/coding/reasoning overrides load the matching GGUF. chat_model_path / agent_model_path optional absolute paths to existing files.
_comment_route_default_to_chat_model: If true, heuristic class 'default' uses the fast chat model when chat_model or models.fast is set.
_comment_auto_pip: Default false: use INSTALL/install.sh or pip manually for optional deps. If true, missing allowlisted packages (e.g. faster-whisper) may be installed automatically in this venv.
_comment_anti_drift_prompt_enabled: When true, inject global minimize-change / anti–AI-drift instructions into the system head.
_comment_direct_feedback_enabled: Opt-in: system head asks for honest, concise critique (still non-clinical; see ETHICAL_AI_PRINCIPLES §11).
_comment_pin_psychology_framework_excerp: When true, Echo/Lilith turns get a short pinned non-clinical interaction-framework reminder (no Chroma required).
_comment_retention_and_caps: Daily cleanup caps for append-only data. All optional; defaults shown.
